message:
Fix nightly clippy lints across all source modules

Comprehensive clippy lint fixes for nightly Rust 1.95.0 across the
entire codebase (cli, core, error, providers, render, rich, storage,
tui, util modules):

Format string interpolation: Replace format!("{}", x) with
format!("{x}") and println!("{}", x) with println!("{x}") throughout
all modules for cleaner inline variable capture.

const fn promotion: Add const to pure functions that qualify:
- BudgetLimits::is_empty, BudgetConfig constructors (global,
  for_provider, override_for_provider)
- ResolvedBudget::has_limits, Cli::effective_format
- Various trivial getters and constructors

if-let chain merging (nightly let-chains feature): Collapse nested
if-let patterns into single if-let-chain expressions:
- Budget violation checks: if let (Some(x), Some(y)) = ... && cond
- Storage write_to_file: if let Some(x) = ... && let Ok(y) = ...
- Multiple similar patterns in providers, render, error modules

Doc comment fixes: Add backticks around identifiers in doc comments
(CodexBar -> `CodexBar`, UsageArgs -> `UsageArgs`, etc.) to satisfy
rustdoc lint requirements.

Clippy allow annotations for acceptable patterns:
- #[allow(clippy::struct_excessive_bools)] on Cli and UsageArgs
- #[allow(clippy::too_many_lines)] on complex functions
- #[allow(clippy::cast_possible_truncation)] on Duration conversions
- #[allow(clippy::cast_precision_loss)] on threshold calculations
- #[allow(clippy::significant_drop_tightening)] in log_capture
- #[allow(clippy::trivially_copy_pass_by_ref)] on const methods
- #[allow(dead_code)] on unused test fixture helpers

Other improvements:
- Add #[must_use] to cache::is_fresh, OfflineCache::get,
  OfflineCache::get_with_staleness
- Add Default impl for OfflineCache
- Add /// # Errors doc sections to all public fallible functions
- Replace .unwrap_or(false) chains with .is_ok_and() idiom
- Replace .map(|v| ...).unwrap_or(false) with .is_ok_and()
- Replace sort_by(|a, b| b.cmp(a)) with sort_by_key(|b| Reverse(...))
- Remove dead code branch in OfflineCacheEntry::staleness
- Use Path::new(".") via unwrap_or_else instead of unwrap_or

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
